examples: Eurasian watermilfoil, a weed not native to Frida Lake, has reproduced prolifically since being accidentally introduced there.
These very short-lived plants reproduce prolifically; that is to say they provide a constant rain of seed in the neighborhood of parent plants.
But Kerry has not been able to raise funds as prolifically as Howard Dean.
Winston Churchill also wrote books, so prolifically in fact that he received his Nobel Prize in literature.
